scope.summary: Implement the FBX joint-animation export bake pass (R-ANIM-039) that writes explicit keyframes at every frame in the export range to prevent interpolation drift when consuming FBX in third-party DCC tools
acceptance_criteria: src/tailor/export/fbx_key_baker.rs exists and exports a bake_joint_animation_fbx function that writes a FBX 7.x ASCII or binary file with one keyframe per frame in the export range for every animated joint
The bake pass is gated behind the tailor-fbx-import feature flag (reusing the FBX dependency already required for import)
Every empty frame in the range receives an explicit keyframe interpolated from the surrounding keys before writing (no gaps that a DCC importer might fill with its own interpolation logic)

The spec note in R-ANIM-039 that this is a post-MVP deliverable is preserved: the MT implements the bake pass but the MVP FBX export MAY omit baked keys with a documented limitation in the ModelManual
scope.risk_if_missed: Without baked keyframes, FBX animation imported into Maya, 3ds Max, or older versions of Blender will be re-interpolated with the host application's own curves, causing visible drift from the Tailor simulation. This is specifically why MD added the auto-key feature in 2026.0.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
